ogTitle: Caldermeade vs California Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Caldermeade and California Gully.
heading: Caldermeade vs California Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Caldermeade, Victoria and California Gully, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Caldermeade has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to California Gully (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Caldermeade or California Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Caldermeade has a flood risk score of 26/100, while California Gully scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Caldermeade scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. California Gully scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Caldermeade or California Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Caldermeade has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
